content.text: The OpenAI Assistants API lets you build persistent, multi-turn AI agents using OpenAI models. You create an
(a configured model with instructions and tools), open
(the reasoning loop that produces replies and calls tools). OpenAI stores threads and messages on its infrastructure — no database needed for conversation memory.

attrs.alt: OpenAI Assistants API: threads, runs, and tools

content.text: a reusable configuration — model, instructions, and tool definitions. Persist until deleted; shared across many threads.
a conversation context. Messages accumulate; the full history is sent to the model on each run.
one execution of the reasoning loop on a thread. Progresses through
built-in tool — generates and executes Python in an OpenAI-managed sandbox.
built-in RAG — attach files to a vector store; the model retrieves relevant chunks automatically.
custom tools described by JSON Schema; your code executes them and returns results to the run.
An assistant is a reusable agent configuration. Define it once and use it across many threads and sessions.
Every conversation is a thread. Add messages to the thread, then create a run to trigger the assistant's reasoning loop:

import time, json

# Create a thread (empty conversation)
thread = client.beta.threads.create()

# Add the first user message
client.beta.threads.messages.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    role="user",
    content="What is the status of order ORD-9001?",
)

# Create a run — triggers the assistant's reasoning loop
run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id,
)

# Poll until the run reaches a terminal state
while run.status in ("queued", "in_progress", "requires_action"):
    time.sleep(1)
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id)

    if run.status == "requires_action":
        # Handle function tool calls
        tool_calls = run.required_action.submit_tool_outputs.tool_calls
        tool_outputs = []
        for tc in tool_calls:
            fn_name = tc.function.name
            fn_args = json.loads(tc.function.arguments)
            if fn_name == "get_order_status":
                result = get_order_status(fn_args["order_id"])
            elif fn_name == "initiate_return":
                result = initiate_return(fn_args["order_id"], fn_args["reason"])
            else:
                result = {"error": f"unknown function {fn_name}"}
            tool_outputs.append({"tool_call_id": tc.id, "output": json.dumps(result)})

        run = client.beta.threads.runs.submit_tool_outputs(
            thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id, tool_outputs=tool_outputs
        )

# Retrieve the assistant's reply
messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id, order="desc")
print(messages.data[0].content[0].text.value)

# Continue the conversation — same thread_id
client.beta.threads.messages.create(thread_id=thread.id, role="user",
    content="Please initiate a return for that order. Reason: wrong size.")
# ... create another run ...

status means the model has decided to call one or more function tools and is waiting for your code to execute them. You must submit tool outputs within the run's timeout (10 minutes by default) or the run expires.
Instead of polling, use the streaming API for real-time output. The
receives typed events as they arrive:
Upload files once, create a vector store, and attach it to the assistant. On each run, the model automatically retrieves relevant chunks when the user asks about document content.

# Upload documents and create a vector store for RAG
with open("return-policy.pdf", "rb") as f:
    file = client.files.create(file=f, purpose="assistants")

vector_store = client.beta.vector_stores.create(
    name="support-docs",
    file_ids=[file.id],
)

# Poll until processing is complete
import time
while vector_store.status == "in_progress":
    time.sleep(2)
    vector_store = client.beta.vector_stores.retrieve(vector_store.id)

# Update the assistant to use this vector store
client.beta.assistants.update(
    assistant.id,
    tool_resources={"file_search": {"vector_store_ids": [vector_store.id]}},
)

# Now any run will automatically:
# 1. Embed the user's query
# 2. Retrieve relevant chunks from the vector store
# 3. Inject chunks into the FM context
# 4. Cite sources in the response (annotations in message content)

# Retrieve citations from the response:
messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id, order="desc")
msg = messages.data[0]
for block in msg.content:
    if hasattr(block, "text"):
        annotations = block.text.annotations
        for ann in annotations:
            if hasattr(ann, "file_citation"):
                print(f"Source: {ann.file_citation.file_id}, quote: {ann.file_citation.quote[:80]}")
Supported file types: PDF, DOCX, TXT, HTML, Markdown, JSON, CSV, PPTX
Vector stores use text-embedding-3-large by default
annotations in message content — include the quote text and file ID
Code Interpreter gives the assistant a sandboxed Python environment. It can parse attached files, perform calculations, and produce charts or processed outputs as downloadable files.

# Code Interpreter: the assistant generates and runs Python in OpenAI's sandbox
# Useful for data analysis, chart generation, file processing

# Attach a CSV file for analysis
with open("sales.csv", "rb") as f:
    data_file = client.files.create(file=f, purpose="assistants")

# Start a thread with the file attached
thread = client.beta.threads.create(
    messages=[{
        "role": "user",
        "content": "Analyse this sales data and plot monthly revenue as a bar chart.",
        "attachments": [{"file_id": data_file.id, "tools": [{"type": "code_interpreter"}]}],
    }]
)
# ... create and run a run ...

# After completion, retrieve generated images:
messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id, order="desc")
for msg in messages.data:
    for block in msg.content:
        if block.type == "image_file":
            img_data = client.files.content(block.image_file.file_id)
            with open("monthly_revenue.png", "wb") as out:
                out.write(img_data.read())
            print("Saved: monthly_revenue.png")
The sandbox includes NumPy, pandas, matplotlib, SciPy, and similar data-science packages
Generated files (PNG, CSV, PDF) are retrievable via
Each run gets an isolated sandbox — no state (variables, files) persists between runs
Managing Costs: Thread Context and File Retention
all messages in a thread are included in every run — a long thread costs more tokens per request. Summarise and start a new thread for long-running conversations.
files, vector stores, and threads accumulate until deleted. Configure
on vector stores to auto-delete after N days.
on the run to control how many tokens of thread history are sent (e.g., limit to the last 20 messages).

# Auto-expire a vector store after 7 days
vector_store = client.beta.vector_stores.create(
    name="temp-docs",
    file_ids=[file.id],
    expires_after={"anchor": "last_active_at", "days": 7},
)

# Truncate thread history to the last 20 messages per run
run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id,
    truncation_strategy={"type": "last_messages", "last_messages": 20},
)
Comparing Assistants API with Direct Chat Completions
Base64 encode or fetch each call

# Files, vector stores, threads, and assistants persist until deleted.
# Clean up after testing to avoid unnecessary storage costs.

client.beta.vector_stores.delete(vector_store.id)
client.files.delete(file.id)
client.beta.threads.delete(thread.id)
client.beta.assistants.delete(assistant.id)
if your poll loop doesn't handle
, the run expires after its timeout and is permanently failed — there is no way to resume an expired run
unlimited threads with thousands of messages become expensive; implement a thread rotation or summarisation strategy in production
after uploading files, the vector store is not immediately ready — poll
arrives as an event — you must call
inside the event handler, not after the stream ends
tool output strings are included in the next model call; very large function outputs (e.g., 10MB JSON blobs) push the context limit — summarise or truncate before returning
Assistants API calls share the same RPM/TPM limits as your OpenAI account tier — high-concurrency apps may need to implement retry logic or upgrade to a higher tier
provides managed conversation memory (threads), built-in tools (Code Interpreter, File Search), and an orchestrated reasoning loop (runs)
store all messages in OpenAI infrastructure; the same thread ID resumes conversations indefinitely until deleted
are the unit of work — create one per user turn; poll or stream to completion; handle
provides managed RAG via vector stores — upload documents once, query automatically on every run
executes sandboxed Python for data analysis and chart generation without infrastructure
by rotating threads, setting vector store expiry, and using
